    1. wrap()

Interface Wrappable<W>

Type Parameters:
W - the type the functional interface returned by wrap()
All Known Subinterfaces:
ExBiConsumer<T,U,E>, ExBiFunction<T,U,R,E>, ExBinaryOperator<T,E>, ExBiPredicate<T,U,E>, ExBooleanSupplier<E>, ExConsumer<T,E>, ExDoubleBinaryOperator<E>, ExDoubleConsumer<E>, ExDoubleFunction<R,E>, ExDoubleMapMultiConsumer<E>, ExDoublePredicate<E>, ExDoubleSupplier<E>, ExDoubleToIntFunction<E>, ExDoubleToLongFunction<E>, ExDoubleUnaryOperator<E>, ExFunction<T,R,E>, ExIntBinaryOperator<E>, ExIntConsumer<E>, ExIntFunction<R,E>, ExIntMapMultiConsumer<E>, ExIntPredicate<E>, ExIntSupplier<E>, ExIntToDoubleFunction<E>, ExIntToLongFunction<E>, ExIntUnaryOperator<E>, ExLongBinaryOperator<E>, ExLongConsumer<E>, ExLongFunction<R,E>, ExLongMapMultiConsumer<E>, ExLongPredicate<E>, ExLongSupplier<E>, ExLongToDoubleFunction<E>, ExLongToIntFunction<E>, ExLongUnaryOperator<E>, ExObjDoubleConsumer<T,E>, ExObjIntConsumer<T,E>, ExObjLongConsumer<T,E>, ExPredicate<T,E>, ExRunnable<E>, ExSupplier<T,E>, ExToDoubleBiFunction<T,U,E>, ExToDoubleFunction<T,E>, ExToIntBiFunction<T,U,E>, ExToIntFunction<T,E>, ExToLongBiFunction<T,U,E>, ExToLongFunction<T,E>, ExUnaryOperator<T,E>
public interface Wrappable<W>
Interfaces which extend this can wrap functional interfaces throwing exceptions using the wrap() method, allowing them to be used in contexts where exceptions are not allowed to be thrown directly.

If a checked exception is thrown, an ExException, which is a runtime exception, will be thrown instead. This will have the original exception as its cause.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @NonNull W
    wrap()
    Wraps this functional interface, allowing it to be used in contexts where exceptions are not allowed to be thrown directly.

  • Method Details

    • wrap

      @NonNull W wrap()
      Wraps this functional interface, allowing it to be used in contexts where exceptions are not allowed to be thrown directly.

      If a checked exception is thrown, an ExException, which is a runtime exception, will be thrown instead. This will have the original exception as its cause.

      Returns:
      a wrapped instance of type W
